  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c_ai", "name": "Artificial Intelligence", "aliases": ["AI"], "description": "Computational techniques that simulate human cognitive capabilities.", "grounding_span": "Artificial Intelligence (AI) refers to the computational techniques that simulate human cognitive capabilities.", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "c_narrow_ai", "name": "Narrow AI", "aliases": ["task-specific AI"], "description": "Present-day AI that is task-specific rather than general.", "grounding_span": "All of the AI in place today is task-specific, or narrow AI.", "location": "Section 1.2"},
    {"id": "c_agi", "name": "Artificial General Intelligence", "aliases": ["AGI"], "description": "AI that achieves general human-level intelligence; not technically possible today.", "grounding_span": "This is known as Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) which, at this point, is not technically possible.", "location": "Section 1.2"},
    {"id": "c_ml", "name": "Machine Learning", "aliases": ["ML"], "description": "Using algorithms that learn by extracting patterns from data.", "grounding_span": "Machine Learning (ML) refers to the field and practice of using algorithms that are able to “learn” by extracting patterns from a large body of data.", "location": "Section 1.2"},
    {"id": "c_supervised", "name": "Supervised learning", "aliases": [], "description": "ML using labeled datasets to classify data or predict outcomes.", "grounding_span": "Supervised learning uses labeled datasets to train algorithms to classify data or predict outcomes.", "location": "Section 1.2"},
    {"id": "c_unsupervised", "name": "Unsupervised learning", "aliases": [], "description": "ML using unlabeled data to discover patterns for clustering or association.", "grounding_span": "Unsupervised learning uses unlabeled data. From that data, it discovers patterns that help solve clustering or association problems.", "location": "Section 1.2"},
    {"id": "c_reinforcement", "name": "Reinforcement learning", "aliases": [], "description": "ML that learns by trial and error, reinforcing successful outcomes.", "grounding_span": "Reinforcement learning sequence of successful outcomes will be reinforced to develop the best recommendation for a given problem.", "location": "Section 1.2"},
    {"id": "c_data_science", "name": "Data science", "aliases": [], "description": "Cross-functional discipline producing data-driven insights and processes.", "grounding_span": "Data science is a broad field that covers a broad range of analytics and computer science techniques.", "location": "Section 1.2"},
    {"id": "c_deep_learning", "name": "Deep learning", "aliases": [], "description": "Computationally intensive ML often requiring GPU acceleration.", "grounding_span": "such as for Deep Learning and GPU acceleration.", "location": "Section 5.1"},
    {"id": "c_neural_networks", "name": "Neural networks", "aliases": ["cognitive architectures"], "description": "Systems designed to think or act like a human.", "grounding_span": "including cognitive architectures and neural networks.", "location": "Section 1.2"},
    {"id": "c_clustering", "name": "Clustering", "aliases": [], "description": "Data mining technique grouping unlabeled data by similarity.", "grounding_span": "Clustering is a data mining technique which groups unlabeled data based on their similarities or differences.", "location": "Section 1.2"},
    {"id": "c_association", "name": "Association", "aliases": [], "description": "Discovering relationships between variables in a dataset.", "grounding_span": "Association is used to discover interesting relationships between variables in a dataset.", "location": "Section 1.2"},
    {"id": "c_dim_reduction", "name": "Dimensionality reduction", "aliases": [], "description": "Reducing dimensions while maintaining meaningful properties.", "grounding_span": "Dimensionality reduction is used to reduce the number of dimensions while still maintaining meaningful properties close to the original data.", "location": "Section 1.2"},
    {"id": "c_predictive_analytics", "name": "Predictive analytics", "aliases": [], "description": "Anticipating results based on output variables.", "grounding_span": "Predictive analytics is used to provide deep insights into various data points and allows for the anticipation of results based on given output variables.", "location": "Section 1.2"},
    {"id": "c_computer_vision", "name": "Computer vision", "aliases": [], "description": "Visual perception tasks such as object recognition.", "grounding_span": "Computer vision: used for visual perception tasks such as object recognition.", "location": "Section 1.2"},
    {"id": "c_anomaly_detection", "name": "Anomaly detection", "aliases": [], "description": "Discovering atypical data points within a dataset.", "grounding_span": "Anomaly detection: used for going through large amounts of data and discovering atypical data points within a dataset.", "location": "Section 1.2"},
    {"id": "c_nlp", "name": "Natural language processing", "aliases": ["NLP"], "description": "Processing language for tasks like translation.", "grounding_span": "and language translation using natural language processing", "location": "Section 1.2"},
    {"id": "c_human_machine_teams", "name": "Human-machine teams", "aliases": [], "description": "Pairing humans and machines each on best-suited tasks.", "grounding_span": "It is more appropriate to think in terms of human-machine teams where each does the tasks for which it is best-suited.", "location": "Section 1.2"},
    {"id": "c_bias", "name": "Bias in AI systems", "aliases": ["harmful bias"], "description": "Bias entering an AI system in many forms.", "grounding_span": "Bias can enter an AI system in many ways.", "location": "Section 3.2"},
    {"id": "c_data_governance", "name": "Data governance", "aliases": [], "description": "Exercise of authority and control over data assets.", "grounding_span": "Data governance is the exercise of authority and control (planning, monitoring, and enforcement) over data assets.", "location": "Section 5.2"},
    {"id": "c_data_lifecycle_mgmt", "name": "Data lifecycle management", "aliases": [], "description": "Managing the value of data throughout its lifecycle.", "grounding_span": "Data lifecycle management is the development, execution, and supervision of plans, policies, programs, and practices that deliver, control, protect, and enhance the value of data and information assets throughout their lifecycles", "location": "Section 5.2"},
    {"id": "c_metadata_tagging", "name": "Metadata tagging", "aliases": ["data card"], "description": "Standardizing metadata captured for new data sources.", "grounding_span": "One example of data lifecycle management is standardizing metadata captured for new data sources by populating a data card used to describe the data.", "location": "Section 5.2"},
    {"id": "c_cdo", "name": "Chief Data Officer", "aliases": ["CDO"], "description": "Executive-branch role mandated by the Evidence Act.", "grounding_span": "requires every executive branch agency to establish a Chief Data Officer (CDO)", "location": "Section 5.2"},
    {"id": "c_ipt", "name": "Integrated Product Team", "aliases": ["IPT"], "description": "Team responsible for implementing AI products, systems, and solutions.", "grounding_span": "The Integrated Product Team (IPT is responsible for implementing AI products, systems, and solutions.", "location": "Section 2.1"},
    {"id": "c_iat", "name": "Integrated Agency Team", "aliases": ["IAT"], "description": "Security, legal, and acquisition support for the IPT.", "grounding_span": "The value of an IAT is to support the IPT members who will actually develop and manage the day-to-day affairs", "location": "Section 2.1"},
    {"id": "c_central_ai_resource", "name": "Central AI technical resource", "aliases": ["central AI resource"], "description": "Hub of AI expertise, tools, and infrastructure support.", "grounding_span": "A central AI technical resource–discussed below–will serve as a hub of AI expertise for program offices to seek the support they need.", "location": "Section 2.1"},
    {"id": "c_ai_practitioner", "name": "AI practitioner", "aliases": [], "description": "The people who do the AI work and need the tools.", "grounding_span": "The people doing the work are the AI practitioners who need the tools to do the work.", "location": "Section 2.1"},
    {"id": "c_responsible_ai", "name": "Responsible and trustworthy AI", "aliases": [], "description": "AI encompassing accuracy, explainability, privacy, safety, bias mitigation.", "grounding_span": "responsibility includes: accuracy, explainability and interpretability, privacy, reliability, robustness, safety, security, the mitigation of harmful bias, and more as the field evolves.", "location": "Section 3.1"},
    {"id": "c_explainability", "name": "Explainability and interpretability", "aliases": [], "description": "Systems providing clear reasoning for outputs.", "grounding_span": "Designing systems that provide clear, understandable reasoning for their outputs.", "location": "Chapter 3"},
    {"id": "c_model_drift", "name": "Model drift", "aliases": ["drift"], "description": "Performance change of a deployed model over time.", "grounding_span": "In production, models can “drift,” meaning that the performance will change over time.", "location": "Section 7.1"},
    {"id": "c_generalization", "name": "Generalization", "aliases": [], "description": "A model's ability to adapt to new, previously unseen data.", "grounding_span": "a process known as generalization, or the model’s ability to adapt properly to new, previously unseen data.", "location": "Section 7.1"},
    {"id": "c_ai_lifecycle", "name": "AI lifecycle", "aliases": [], "description": "Iterative process from business problem to AI solution.", "grounding_span": "The AI lifecycle is the iterative process of moving from a business problem to an AI solution that solves that problem.", "location": "Section 7.1"},
    {"id": "c_use_case", "name": "AI use case", "aliases": [], "description": "A specific challenge or opportunity AI may solve.", "grounding_span": "A use case is a specific challenge or opportunity that AI may solve.", "location": "Section 7.3"},
    {"id": "c_buy_or_build", "name": "Buy or build", "aliases": ["buy vs. build"], "description": "Decision on acquiring vs. developing AI capability.", "grounding_span": "Answering the “buy or build” question depends on the program office’s function and the nature of the commercial offering.", "location": "Section 7.4"},
    {"id": "c_te", "name": "Test and Evaluation", "aliases": ["T&E"], "description": "Providing knowledge to manage risk in developing and sustaining systems.", "grounding_span": "The basic purpose of Test & Evaluation (T&E) is to provide knowledge to manage risk that’s involved in developing, producing, operating, and sustaining systems and capabilities.", "location": "Section 7.4"},
    {"id": "c_dte", "name": "Developmental Test and Evaluation", "aliases": ["DT&E"], "description": "Verifies a system meets technical performance specifications.", "grounding_span": "First is developmental test and evaluation (DT&E), which verifies that a system meets technical performance specifications.", "location": "Section 7.4"},
    {"id": "c_ote", "name": "Operational Test and Evaluation", "aliases": ["OT&E"], "description": "Testing under realistic operational conditions with the operator.", "grounding_span": "In time, operational test and evaluation (OT&E) follows.", "location": "Section 7.4"},
    {"id": "c_ethical_te", "name": "Ethical T&E", "aliases": [], "description": "Ensuring the system does only what it is supposed to.", "grounding_span": "Depending on the AI solution’s purpose, this step ensures the system does only what it’s supposed to do and doesn’t do what it’s not supposed to do.", "location": "Section 7.4"},
    {"id": "c_model_te", "name": "Model T&E", "aliases": [], "description": "Running the model on test data and scoring performance.", "grounding_span": "In this step, the model is run on the test data and its performance is scored based on metrics identified in the test planning process.", "location": "Section 7.4"},
    {"id": "c_devsecops", "name": "DevSecOps", "aliases": [], "description": "Integrated practice of software development, IT operations, and security.", "grounding_span": "DevSecOps is the integrated practice of bringing together software development, IT operations, and the security team.", "location": "Section 5.1"},
    {"id": "c_iac", "name": "Infrastructure as Code", "aliases": ["IaC"], "description": "Bringing software-development discipline to infrastructure management.", "grounding_span": "Infrastructure as code (IaC) brings the repeatability, transparency, and testing of modern software development to the management of infrastructure such as networks, load balancers, virtual machines, Kubernetes clusters, and monitoring.", "location": "Section 7.4"},
    {"id": "c_cloud_platform", "name": "Cloud platform", "aliases": ["cloud & infrastructure"], "description": "Elastic storage and compute underpinning many AI tools.", "grounding_span": "Many AI tools and solutions are tied to a cloud platform.", "location": "Section 5.1"},
    {"id": "c_automl", "name": "AutoML", "aliases": [], "description": "Tools automating model training and deployment.", "grounding_span": "AutoML tools—tools that automate many of the model training and deployment processes—are an emerging area of AI tool development.", "location": "Section 5.1"},
    {"id": "c_peopleops", "name": "PeopleOps", "aliases": [], "description": "Recruit, develop, retain, and organize an AI-ready workforce.", "grounding_span": "Recruit, develop, retain, and organize an AI-ready workforce.", "location": "Section 6.3"},
    {"id": "c_cloudops", "name": "CloudOps", "aliases": [], "description": "Provide and allocate storage, compute, and other cloud resources.", "grounding_span": "Provide and allocate storage, compute, and other resources in the cloud.", "location": "Section 6.3"},
    {"id": "c_secops", "name": "SecOps", "aliases": [], "description": "Security best practices and protection of AI techniques from attacks.", "grounding_span": "SecOps unifies best practices in security for software development, system administration, data access, and protection of AI techniques from attacks.", "location": "Section 6.3"},
    {"id": "c_devops", "name": "DevOps", "aliases": [], "description": "Securely moving software into production with CI/CD.", "grounding_span": "A mature AI organization must be able to securely move software into a production environment and provide continuous integration and delivery of software updates.", "location": "Section 6.3"},
    {"id": "c_dataops", "name": "DataOps", "aliases": [], "description": "Enabling data discovery, access, and use for AI development.", "grounding_span": "To deliver data to development teams, DataOps enables data discovery, access, and use for AI development.", "location": "Section 6.3"},
    {"id": "c_mlops", "name": "MLOps", "aliases": [], "description": "Selection, deployment, and maintenance of ML models in an AI system.", "grounding_span": "MLOps is the selection, application, interpretation, deployment, and maintenance of machine learning models within an AI-enabled system.", "location": "Section 6.3"},
    {"id": "c_aiops", "name": "AIOps", "aliases": [], "description": "AI capability development as systems engineering and product management.", "grounding_span": "AIOps support AI capability development as a discipline of systems engineering and product management.", "location": "Section 6.3"},
    {"id": "c_five_vs", "name": "Five Vs", "aliases": ["Volume, Velocity, Veracity, Variety, Variability"], "description": "Descriptive metadata dimensions for datasets.", "grounding_span": "metadata can be applied to datasets that detail information on the Five Vs", "location": "Section 5.2"},
    {"id": "c_soo", "name": "Statement of Objectives", "aliases": ["SOO"], "description": "Acquisition vehicle for uncertain solution paths.", "grounding_span": "Use a Statement of Objectives (SOO) when you are less certain of a solution’s path and want to consider innovative or unorthodox methods.", "location": "Section 7.4"},
    {"id": "c_pws", "name": "Performance Work Statement", "aliases": ["PWS"], "description": "Acquisition vehicle for clear specifications with measurable standards.", "grounding_span": "Consider using a Performance Work Statement (PWS) when you have clear specifications on what the product or service needs to do.", "location": "Section 7.4"},
    {"id": "c_cots", "name": "Commercial-off-the-shelf", "aliases": ["COTS"], "description": "Ready-made commercial products for some AI applications.", "grounding_span": "Some applications, such as voice recognition, can be deployed from commercial-of-the-shelf (COTS) products.", "location": "Section 1.2"},
    {"id": "c_org_maturity", "name": "Organizational maturity areas", "aliases": [], "description": "Capacity to embed AI capabilities across the organization.", "grounding_span": "Organizational maturity areas represent the capacity to embed AI capabilities across the organization.", "location": "Section 6.2"},
    {"id": "c_op_maturity", "name": "Operational maturity areas", "aliases": [], "description": "Organizational functions impacting implementation of AI capabilities.", "grounding_span": "Operational maturity areas represent organizational functions that impact the implementation of AI capabilities.", "location": "Section 6.3"},
    {"id": "c_initial", "name": "Initial / Ad Hoc", "aliases": [], "description": "Process is unpredictable, poorly controlled, and unmanaged.", "grounding_span": "Process is unpredictable, poorly controlled, and unmanaged.", "location": "Section 6.2"},
    {"id": "c_repeatable", "name": "Repeatable", "aliases": [], "description": "Documented, repeatable steps with consistent results.", "grounding_span": "Process is documented and steps are repeatable with consistent results. Often reactionary.", "location": "Section 6.2"},
    {"id": "c_defined", "name": "Defined", "aliases": [], "description": "Documented, standardized processes improved over time.", "grounding_span": "Documented, standardized processes that are improved over time.", "location": "Section 6.2"},
    {"id": "c_managed", "name": "Managed", "aliases": [], "description": "Activities measured with active, evidence-based management.", "grounding_span": "Activities are effectively measured, and meeting objectives are based on active management and evidence-based outcomes.", "location": "Section 6.2"},
    {"id": "c_optimized", "name": "Optimized", "aliases": [], "description": "Continuous performance improvement through innovation.", "grounding_span": "Continuous performance improvement of activities through incremental and innovative improvements.", "location": "Section 6.2"},
    {"id": "c_data_analyst", "name": "Data analyst", "aliases": [], "description": "Answers routine operational questions with data analysis.", "grounding_span": "focuses on answering routine operational questions using well-established data analysis techniques, including AI tools.", "location": "Section 4.3"},
    {"id": "c_data_engineer", "name": "Data engineer", "aliases": [], "description": "Builds data science/AI tools for reliability and scale.", "grounding_span": "focuses on carefully building and engineering data science and AI tools for reliability, accuracy, and scale.", "location": "Section 4.3"},
    {"id": "c_data_scientist_role", "name": "Data scientist", "aliases": [], "description": "Designs data science/AI models, tools, and techniques.", "grounding_span": "focuses on thoughtfully and rigorously designing data science/AI models, tools, and techniques.", "location": "Section 4.3"},
    {"id": "c_tpm", "name": "Technical program manager", "aliases": [], "description": "Manages software development teams building AI tools.", "grounding_span": "manages software development teams, including teams building AI tools and capabilities.", "location": "Section 4.3"},
    {"id": "c_ai_champion", "name": "AI champion", "aliases": [], "description": "Advocates for the AI solution's value.", "grounding_span": "Advocates for the AI solution’s value", "location": "Section 4.3"}
  ],
  "definitions": [
    {"id": "d_ai_intro", "term": "Artificial Intelligence", "verbatim_text": "Artificial Intelligence (AI) refers to the computational techniques that simulate human cognitive capabilities.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2026-07-04", "grounding_span": "Artificial Intelligence (AI) refers to the computational techniques that simulate human cognitive capabilities.", "location": "Introduction"},

    {"id": "d_data_science", "term": "Data science", "verbatim_text": "Data science is the practice and methodology of implementing analytics or machine learning techniques, with subject matter expertise, to provide insights that lead to actions.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2026-07-04", "grounding_span": "Data science is the practice and methodology of implementing analytics or machine learning techniques, with subject matter expertise, to provide insights that lead to actions.", "location": "Section 1.2"},

    {"id": "d_supervised", "term": "Supervised learning", "verbatim_text": "also known as supervised machine learning, is defined by its use of labeled datasets to train algorithms to classify data or predict outcomes accurately.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2026-07-04", "grounding_span": "also known as supervised machine learning, is defined by its use of labeled datasets to train algorithms to classify data or predict outcomes accurately.", "location": "Section 1.2"},
    {"id": "d_reinforcement", "term": "Reinforcement learning", "verbatim_text": "is a behavioral machine learning model that is similar to supervised learning, but the algorithm isn’t trained using sample data.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2026-07-04", "grounding_span": "is a behavioral machine learning model that is similar to supervised learning, but the algorithm isn’t trained using sample data.", "location": "Section 1.2"},
